name: Pure Functions
def: Functions whose output depends only on inputs and that produce no observable side effects.
problem: Make code deterministic and locally reasoned about, independent of global state and order of evaluation.
mechanism: No mutation of external state, no I/O; same inputs always yield same outputs (referential transparency).
limits: Threading state through purely can be verbose
failures: "Pure" functions that secretly log or read clocks
whenNot: I/O boundary code (isolate, don’t pretend purity)
myths: "Pure = slow" — purity enables optimisation and caching
sectors: All sectors — a discipline, not a feature
